sections.label: Section 612b
sections.translation: It is not lawful for you to mention your mother .

sections.label: Section 613
sections.translation: O my city !

sections.label: Section 613b
sections.original: μολὼν ἐς Ἄργος ἀνακάλει Λέρνης ὕδωρ .
sections.translation: Go to Argos , and invoke the waters of Lerna .

sections.label: Section 614
sections.original: εἶμι , μὴ πόνει · σὲ δʼ αἰνῶ , μῆτερ .
sections.translation: I will ; do not be troubled ; but I thank you , mother .

sections.label: Section 614b
sections.translation: Go forth from the land !

sections.label: Section 615
sections.original: ἔξιμεν · πατέρα δέ μοι δὸς εἰσιδεῖν .
sections.translation: I will go ; but let me see my father .

sections.label: Section 615b
sections.translation: You will not have your wish .

sections.label: Section 616
sections.translation: At least then my maiden sisters .

sections.label: Section 616b
sections.translation: You will not ever see them either .

sections.label: Section 617
sections.translation: Ah , my sisters !

sections.label: Section 617b
sections.translation: Why do you , their bitterest enemy , call on them ?

sections.label: Section 618
sections.translation: To you at least farewell , mother !

sections.label: Section 618b
sections.translation: Indeed I am faring well , my son !

sections.label: Section 619
sections.translation: I am no longer your son .

sections.label: Section 619b
sections.translation: I was born to great sorrow .

sections.label: Section 620
sections.translation: Because my brother treats me outrageously .

sections.label: Section 620b
sections.translation: I am treated just the same .

sections.label: Section 621
sections.translation: Where will you be stationed before the towers ?

sections.label: Section 621b
sections.translation: Why do you ask me this ?

sections.label: Section 622
sections.translation: I will set myself against you for your death .

sections.label: Section 622b
sections.translation: I too have the same desire .

sections.label: Section 623
sections.original: ὦ τάλαινʼ ἐγώ · τί δράσετʼ , ὦ τέκνα ;
sections.translation: Woe is me ! what will you do , my sons ?

sections.label: Section 623b
sections.translation: The event will show .

sections.label: Section 624
sections.translation: Oh , try to escape your father ’ s curse ! Exit Jocasta .

sections.label: Section 624b
sections.translation: May destruction seize our whole house !

sections.label: Section 625
sections.original: ὡς τάχʼ οὐκέθʼ αἱματηρὸν τοὐμὸν ἀργήσει ξίφος .
sections.translation: Soon my sword will be busy , plunged in gore . But I call my native land and the gods to witness , with what dishonor and bitter treatment I am being driven forth , as though I were a slave , not a son of Oedipus as much as he . If anything happens to you , my city , blame him , not me ;

sections.label: Section 630
sections.original: οὐχ ἑκὼν γὰρ ἦλθον , ἄκων δʼ ἐξελαύνομαι χθονός .
sections.translation: for I did not come willingly , and unwillingly I am driven from the land . And you , Phoebus , lord of highways , and my home , farewell , and my comrades , and statues of the gods , where sheep are sacrificed . For I do not know if I can ever again address you ; though hope is not yet asleep , which makes me confident that with the gods ’ help

sections.label: Section 635
sections.original: τόνδʼ ἀποκτείνας κρατήσειν τῆσδε Θηβαίας χθονός .
sections.translation: I shall slay him and rule this land of Thebes . Exit Polyneices .

sections.label: Section 636
sections.original: ἔξιθʼ ἐκ χώρας · ἀληθῶς δʼ ὄνομα Πολυνείκη πατὴρ
sections.translation: Get out of the country ! It was a true name our father gave you , when , prompted by some god , he called you Polyneices , man of many quarrels . Exit Eteocles .

sections.label: Section 1.638
sections.translation: Cadmus of Tyre came to this land , and at his feet a four-footed ,

sections.label: Section 1.640
sections.translation: untamed heifer threw itself down , fulfilling an oracle , where the god ’ s prophecy told him to make his home in the plains rich with wheat ,

sections.label: Section 1.645
sections.translation: and where the lovely waters of Dirce pour over the fields , the green and deep-seeded fields ; here Bromius ’ mother gave birth

sections.label: Section 1.650
sections.translation: from her union with Zeus ; Bromius , round whom the ivy twined its wreaths while he was still a baby , covering him and blessing him in the shades of its green foliage ,

sections.label: Section 1.655
sections.translation: a Bacchic dance for the maids and wives inspired in Thebes .

sections.label: Section 1.657
sections.translation: There was Ares ’ murderous dragon , a savage guard ,

sections.label: Section 1.660
sections.translation: watching with wandering eye the watery rivers and fresh streams . Cadmus destroyed it with a jagged stone , when he came there to draw lustral water ; smiting the deadly head

sections.label: Section 1.665
sections.translation: with a blow of his beast-slaying arm ; and by the counsel of PalIas , the motherless goddess , he cast the teeth upon the deep fields to fall to the earth ,

sections.label: Section 1.670
sections.translation: from which the earth brought forth a sight fully-armed , above the surface of the soil ; but grim slaughter once again united them to the earth they loved , bedewing with blood the ground that had

sections.label: Section 1.675
sections.translation: shown them to the sunlit breath of heaven .

sections.label: Section 676
sections.translation: And you , Epaphus , born from Io , our first mother , and child of Zeus : you I summon in foreign cry ,

sections.label: Section 680
sections.translation: oh ! in foreign prayers : come , come to this land ; your descendants settled here ; and the goddesses of twofold name , Persephone and the kindly

sections.label: Section 685
sections.translation: goddess Demeter the queen of all , Earth the nurse of all , won it for themselves ; send to the help of this land those torch-bearing goddesses ; for to gods all things are easy .

sections.label: Section 690
sections.original: χώρει σὺ καὶ κόμιζε τὸν Μενοικέως
sections.translation: Go , bring Creon , son of Menoeceus , the brother of Jocasta my mother ; tell him I want to consult with him on matters public and private , before we set out to battle and the arrangement of the army .

sections.label: Section 695
sections.original: καίτοι ποδῶν σῶν μόχθον ἐκλύει παρών ·
sections.translation: But he is here , saving you the trouble ; I see him on his way to my house .

sections.label: Section 697
sections.original: ἦ πόλλʼ ἐπῆλθον εἰσιδεῖν χρῄζων σʼ , ἄναξ
sections.translation: I have been everywhere , lord Eteocles , in my desire to see you , and have gone all round the gates and sentinels of Thebes hunting for you .

sections.label: Section 700
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ἐγὼ σʼ ἔχρῃζον εἰσιδεῖν , Κρέον ·
sections.translation: And I wanted to see you , Creon ; for I found the terms of peace far from satisfactory , when I came to confer with Polyneices .

sections.label: Section 703
sections.original: ἤκουσα μεῖζον αὐτὸν ἢ Θήβας φρονεῖν ,
sections.translation: I hear that he has wider aims than Thebes , relying on his alliance with Adrastus and his army . But we must leave this dependent on the gods ;

sections.label: Section 705
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἐς θεοὺς χρὴ ταῦτʼ ἀναρτήσαντʼ ἔχειν ·
sections.translation: I have come to tell you our chief obstacle .

sections.label: Section 707
sections.original: τὰ ποῖα ταῦτα ; τὸν λόγον γὰρ ἀγνοῶ .
sections.translation: What is that ? I do not understand what you say .

sections.label: Section 708
sections.translation: Someone has come who was captured from the Argives .

sections.label: Section 709
sections.original: λέγει δὲ δὴ τί τῶν ἐκεῖ νεώτερον ;
sections.translation: What news does he bring from there ?

sections.label: Section 710
sections.translation: He says the Argive army intend at once to wind about the city of Thebes and its towers , with their army .

sections.label: Section 712
sections.translation: In that case the city of Cadmus must lead out its army .

sections.label: Section 713
sections.original: ποῖ ; μῶν νεάζων οὐχ ὁρᾷς ἃ χρή σʼ ὁρᾶν ;
sections.translation: Where ? Are you so young that your eyes do not see what they should ?

sections.label: Section 714
sections.original: ἐκτὸς τάφρων τῶνδʼ , ὡς μαχουμένους τάχα .
sections.translation: Across those trenches , to fight at once .

sections.label: Section 715
sections.original: σμικρὸν τὸ πλῆθος τῆσδε γῆς , οἳ δʼ ἄφθονοι .
sections.translation: Our forces are small , while theirs are plentiful .

sections.label: Section 716
sections.original: ἐγᾦδα κείνους τοῖς λόγοις ὄντας θρασεῖς .
sections.translation: I know well they are brave in argument .

sections.label: Section 717
sections.original: ἔχει τινʼ ὄγκον τἄργος Ἑλλήνων πάρα .
sections.translation: Argos has some weight among the Hellenes .

sections.label: Section 718
sections.original: θάρσει · τάχʼ αὐτῶν πεδίον ἐμπλήσω φόνου .
sections.translation: Never fear ! I will soon fill the plain with their dead .

sections.label: Section 719
sections.original: θέλοιμʼ ἄν · ἀλλὰ τοῦθʼ ὁρῶ πολλοῦ πόνου .
sections.translation: I could wish it so ; but I see great difficulties in this .
